response.justification: Candidate is a strong generalist software craftsman, but the resume shows minimal recent C++ work (a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015) and no Windows-platform focus, no SQL Server/Oracle experience, and no Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain exposure. Core stack is JS/TypeScript, DLang, and Node.js — a poor match for a mid-senior C++/Windows/fixed-income role.
strengths: Long, varied software engineering career with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Some prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on a developer-tooling product
Solid communication skills evidenced by talks, workshops, and teaching engagements
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience, ~10 years ago — far short of the required 5–10 years
No demonstrated Windows-platform development, SQL Server/Oracle, or Summit experience
Zero capital markets, investment banking, or fixed-income domain background
